description: Conceptual diagram illustrating the "Assignment of class codes at 20m according to defined aggregation rules," depicted as step 3 in a larger process. The diagram shows a grid of cells, each representing a 20-metre resolution unit. Within this grid, cells are assigned different class codes represented by colours: red and green. A prominent red-coloured area forms a hollow shape on the left, covering a 4x5 block of cells, but with a 2x2 inner block of unclassified (white) cells. To the right and below this red area, a larger green-coloured area spans across multiple cells. A smaller, isolated 1x2 block of green cells is also located at the bottom-centre of the red area. Several white, unclassified cells are also distributed throughout the grid. The diagram suggests an initial classification or aggregation process where specific land cover classes are assigned to 20m grid cells. Text below the image, partially visible, indicates a subsequent step: "4) Unlabelled areas are to be filled by proximity if below the MMU".
